input.background: Yay-Sachs is a metabolic disorder that is inherited as an autosomal recessive trait. Both recessive alleles are necessary for expression of the disease. Therefore, when each parent is a carrier, there is a one in four chance of transmitting the genetic disorder to each offspring.
input.question: A carrier couple is unaware of their carrier state. Let X represent the number of children conceived before an affected offspring is encountered. Is X is a binomial random variable? Explain.

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
output.answer: This is not a binomial because the number of trials $n$ is not fixed.

X.description: Number of children conceived before an affected offspring is encountered.

CarrierState.description: The carrier state of the parents, which affects the probability of offspring being affected.

AffectedOffspringProbability.description: Probability of an offspring being affected when both parents are carriers.
